name: Kompyte Competitive Intelligence API
what_it_does: Automated competitive intelligence platform (part of Semrush) with REST API — monitoring competitor websites, social media, ads, and review sites for changes, automatically generating and updating sales battlecards, and delivering competitive insights to sales and product teams.
use_cases: Querying competitor change alerts and news for sales team notification via API
Pulling competitor battlecard content for embedding in sales enablement tools
Monitoring competitor pricing page changes and feature updates via webhook
Syncing win/loss competitive data with CRM for deal analysis
Integrating Kompyte competitive insights into Slack channels for real-time alerts
not_for: Primary market research and qualitative competitive analysis
SEO and search competitive analysis (use Semrush directly for that)
Small companies without structured competitive intelligence programs
best_when: A B2B company wants automated monitoring of 5-50 competitors across their digital presence with auto-generated battlecards and real-time change alerts, within the Semrush ecosystem.
avoid_when: You need deep qualitative win/loss analysis, or you already use Klue or Crayon with existing content — switching costs are high.

auth.notes: API key from Kompyte account settings. Bearer token auth. Workspace-scoped access. Webhook for competitor change alerts. Part of Semrush platform — may share authentication infrastructure. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes.

pricing.paid_tiers: Part of Semrush Enterprise; contact sales
pricing.notes: Acquired by Semrush in 2021. Originally Spanish company (Barcelona). Now part of Semrush's competitive intelligence suite. Enterprise pricing as part of Semrush. Automatic competitor tracking across web, social, ads, SEO, job postings. Battle card auto-generation feature. Semrush data integration.

performance.rate_limits: Enterprise rate limits per Semrush contract

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for competitors, alerts, battlecards, and insights. Bearer token auth. Limited public API documentation — primarily partner-facing. Kompyte-specific API vs Semrush API distinction important. Competitor monitoring setup required in platform before API data is available.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Alert reads idempotent. Battlecard reads idempotent. Competitor change data idempotent by event ID. Configuration reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: API documentation is sparse — primarily built for partner integrations post-acquisition
Semrush integration may affect which API endpoint to use — clarify with support
Competitor tracking must be configured in Kompyte UI before API data is available
Battlecard auto-generation may lag behind actual competitor changes
Semrush enterprise plan required — standalone Kompyte plans may have limited API

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR and SOC2 compliant. EU and US data residency. Bearer token auth. Part of Semrush enterprise security program. Competitive intelligence data is sensitive — secure key storage important. Semrush acquisition may affect data handling policies.
